iti15:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti15:2.1:
“Monks, I don’t envision even one other fetter–
fettered by which beings conjoined go wandering & transmigrating on for a long, long time–like the fetter of craving.
iti15:2.2: Fettered with the fetter of craving, beings conjoined go wandering & transmigrating on for a long, long time.”
iti15:3.1: With craving his companion, a man
iti15:3.2: wanders on a long, long time.
iti15:3.3: Neither in this state here nor anywhere else
iti15:3.4: does he go beyond the wandering-on.
iti15:4.2: that craving brings stress into play–
iti15:4.3: free from craving, devoid of clinging,
iti15:4.4: mindful, the monk lives the mendicant life.
